film.title: Wall Street
film.overview: A young and impatient stockbroker is willing to do anything to get to the top, including trading on illegal inside information taken through a ruthless and greedy corporate raider, whom takes the youth under his wing.

dialogues.dialogue: like some p**s-poor South American country.  I strongly recommend you  to see through Mr. Gekko's shameless intention here  to strip this company  and severely penalize the stockholders!
dialogues.cause_raw: intending to harm the company and its stockholders

dialogues.dialogue: I got to live in Manhattan to be a player.  There's no nobility in poverty anymore.  One day you'll be proud of me.  It's yourself you got to be proud of, huckleberry.  How much you need?
There's no nobility in poverty anymore.  One day you'll be proud of me.  It's yourself you got to be proud of, huckleberry.  How much you need?  Can you spare 300?
Mr. Fox.  His attorney, Mr. Saul.  I'd be proud to have a son like Buddy.  I thought this was an informal gathering.  What's your attorney doing here?
O.K.  Thank you.  I got to go.  I'm proud of you.  Thanks.  The stock's at 191/4,  and it's going up.
